IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: Provide behavioral health, support for older adults, social justice/anti hate, HIV/AIDS related, an extensive array of homeless, substance abuse and health access initiatives and services to vulnerable communities.
IRS990.MissionDesc: Special Service for Groups, ("SSG"), is a non-profit organization dedicated to providing community-based solutions to the social and economic issues facing those in the greatest need. SSG has evolved into a model organization which is designed to provide service to diverse groups with maximum efficiency and impact. This is achieved by developing and managing programs which serve our many communities by encouraging their involvement and self-sufficiency. SSG believes that the needs of groups and individuals cross traditional ethnic, racial, and other cultural boundaries. SSG serves as a bridge between people with common needs to identify ways to pool resources for the greatest good of all.
IRS990.Desc: Homeless Services: Street outreach to homeless individuals, youth and families. Services include permanent and transitional housing placement, emergency shelter, case management, job training and transportation assistance. In addition, Homeless Services Programs includes a full service portal to serve individuals/families in South L.A. 18,000 individuals and 3,200 households were served in the fiscal year.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: Mental Health Services: Prevention education and intervention, forensic social services, emotional and psychosocial support, outpatient mental health counseling service, assessment and follow-up treatment for children and family mental illness and substance abuse. 33,500 individuals were served in the fiscal year.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: Job and Education: Access to Prevention Advocacy Intervention and Treatment (APAIT) is the most comprehensive provider of HIV/AIDS services for API in Southern California. Its history is rooted in advocacy and provides critical services to the most vulnerable in the community during times of crisis and when other support systems are difficult to access. APAIT was able to raise awareness about mental health and help educate individuals in the spirit of community.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SSG is exempt from taxation under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(3) and California Revenue and Taxation Code Section 23701d. Generally accepted accounting principles provide accounting and disclosure guidance about positions taken by an organization in its tax returns that might be uncertain. Management has considered its tax positions and believes that all of the positions taken by SSG in its federal and state exempt organization tax returns are more likely than not to be sustained upon examination. SSG's returns are subject to examination by federal and state taxing authorities, generally for three and four years, respectively, after they are filed.

The California Conference For Equality And Justice
Trusted Caregiver And Consulting Services LLC

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: Homeless and supportive services for families

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SSG's Homeless Outreach Project Integrated Care System (HOPIC) is an established and successful community-based service provider targeting Service Planning Area 6 (SPA 6) of Los Angeles - which includes South Los Angeles and surrounding communities. HOPICS successfully operates a range of homeless service programs including behavioral health, re-entry, housing and supportive services to individuals and families in need. HOPICS is the designated Coordinated Entry System (CES) entry point for SPA 6 - linking clients to appropriate services within their network of community partner agencies. The criteria for selection of community partner agencies (i.e. other nonprofits) includes being in good standing with primary funder - LA Housing Services Authority. The criteria for housing partners/hotel vouchers is based on location and vacancy. All units must pass safety and health inspections.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: When the Form 990 has been prepared, Special Service for Groups' Fiscal Director sends each member of the Board of Directors (BOD) a copy of the Form 990 to review prior to the next scheduled BOD meeting. At BOD monthly meeting, the BOD reviews and approves the Form 990 for filing.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: An acknowledgement of Special Service for Groups' Conflict of Interest Policy is signed annually by all Board of Director members and key employees.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Board of Directors annually reviews the Executive Director and approves any compensation increase. Key employees are reviewed by the Executive Director on an annual basis. All salaries are determined by comparable positions in the non-profit industry. Special Service for Groups belongs to an organization (employers group) which publishes the salary survey used to determine the comparable salaries.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Special Service for Groups makes its governing documents, conflict of interest policies and financial statements available to all those interested in such matters upon request. Board of Directors of SSG assumes responsibility for oversight of annual audit and recommends selection of independent accountant.
Pension plan related changes other than net periodic pension cost 2,045,030.
